briefdescription.para: Lazy cache for large signals (N >= threshold).
Chained-block pool for connection nodes.
Head of emission-order doubly-linked list (index)
Tail of emission-order doubly-linked list (index)
Number of active connections.
Slots nulled during emit, pending cleanup.
Used to guard against nested Emit() calls.
Used to guard against deletion during Emit() calls.
Constructor. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Virtual destructor. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Queries whether there are any connected slots.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
True if there are any slots connected to the signal.

briefdescription.para: Queries the number of slots.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The number of slots connected to this signal

briefdescription.para: Emits a signal with parameter pack.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.33
args
The parameter pack
The value returned by the last callback
Cannot be called from inside the same Signal's Emit methods.

briefdescription.para: Emits a signal with parameter pack.

args

briefdescription.para: Called by Signal implementations, when the user calls Signal.Connect( ... ).

Called by Signal implementations, when the user calls Signal.Connect( ... ).

briefdescription.para: This method is called when the slot is disconnecting.
Helper to find whether a callback is connected.

Pointer to the SignalConnectionNode, or nullptr if not found

briefdescription.para: Deletes a connection by node pointer.
Helper to remove NULL items from pool, which is only safe at the end of Emit() i.e. not from methods which can be called during a signal Emit(), such as Disconnect(). @SINCE_1_0.0.
Ensure the hash map cache exists and is populated (for large signals). @SINCE_2_5.17.
Append a node to the tail of the emission-order linked list. @SINCE_2_5.17.
Unlink a node from the emission-order linked list. @SINCE_2_5.17.
Deleted copy constructor, signals don't support copying. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Deleted move constructor, signals don't support moving. @SINCE_1_9.25.
Deleted copy assignment operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Deleted move assignment operator. @SINCE_1_9.25.
Implementation class for Dali::Signal.

detaileddescription.para: A slot can be connected to many signals A signal can be connected to many slots
To provide automatic disconnection when either a signal or the object owning the slot dies, observers are used.
A signal is an object with state. It holds a list of SignalConnections.

E.g. Signal OnTouch. mSignalConnections contains
Callback 0
Signal Observer 0
Callback 1
Signal Observer 1
Callback 2
Signal Observer 2
OnTouch.Emit() will run callbacks 0, 1 and 2.
When the signal is destroyed. SignalDisconnected() is called on each Signal Observer.

Slots are just static or member functions, so have no state. E.g. they can't keep track of how many signals they are connected to. If the object owning a slot dies, it must automatically be disconnected from all signals. If it doesn't disconnect and the signal is emitted, there will be a crash.
To keep track of connections between slots and signals, a Connection tracker is used. It holds a list of SlotConnections.

When the connection tracker is destroyed, SlotDisconnected() is called on every slot observer ( signal ). Signals implement the Slot Observer interface, to be told when a slot has disconnected. Connection tracker implements the Signal Observer interface, to be told when a signal has disconnected (died).
@SINCE_1_0.0
